nodes.title: Delays
nodes.oneLiner: The gap between doing something and seeing what happens - the source of most bad decisions in systems.

nodes.title: Emergence
nodes.oneLiner: When the whole does something none of the parts could do alone - behaviour that arises from interactions, not instructions.

nodes.title: Exponential Growth
nodes.oneLiner: When something grows by a percentage rather than an amount - it feels slow until it suddenly feels unstoppable.

nodes.title: Feedback loops
nodes.oneLiner: Reinforcing loops accelerate change, balancing loops resist it - together they drive all system behaviour.

nodes.title: Fixes that fail
nodes.oneLiner: A quick fix that addresses the symptom but makes the underlying problem worse over time.

nodes.title: Shifting the Burden
nodes.oneLiner: Relying on a quick fix so much that the ability to do the real thing slowly disappears.

nodes.title: Unintended Consequences
nodes.oneLiner: Every intervention in a system produces effects you didn't plan for - sometimes bigger than the ones you did.

edges.note: "Delays in feedback loops are what make systems overshoot and oscillate"
"Most fixes fail because the delay between action and consequence hides the damage"
"Delays mean consequences arrive long after the decision that caused them"
"Delays in recognising exponential growth are why it catches people off guard"
"Feedback loops are the mechanism through which emergence often arises"
"Emergent behaviour is often unintended - the system produces what nobody designed"
"Trying to control emergent behaviour with top-down fixes often backfires"
"Dependency patterns emerge from repeated interactions, not from any single decision"
"Exponential growth is a reinforcing feedback loop left unchecked"
"Exponential growth often produces unintended consequences as it outstrips the system's ability to cope"
"Attempting to fix an exponential problem with a linear solution will always fall behind"
Most fixes that fail involve a balancing loop (the fix) that triggers a delayed reinforcing loop (the side effect).
Consequences travel through feedback loops you didn't know existed.
Fixes that fail often lead to shifting the burden - the quick fix becomes the default, and the real solution atrophies.
Every fix that fails is a case study in unintended consequences.
"The reinforcing loop between quick-fix reliance and real-solution atrophy drives the pattern"
"The real solution usually has a longer delay, which makes the quick fix more attractive"
